data.title: US renewable energy market expected to reach $1,002.13 GW by 2033 from $429.55 GW in 2024, with a 3.64% CAGR. Leading states include California, Texas, and New York with distinct strategies.
data.description: A report on the United States Renewable Energy Market projects it to reach US$1,002.13 Gigawatt by 2033, increasing from US$429.55 Gigawatt in 2024 at a CAGR of 3.64% from 2025 to 2033. The market shows ongoing growth in solar, wind, hydro, and emerging green technologies, indicating a positive trend in the U.S. renewable energy sector. The report is now available on ResearchAndMarkets.com for further insights, detailing aspects such as hydro power, end user analysis, state breakdown, and company assessments.

data.title: Bitcoin's price prediction anticipates a surge to $138,000 as bullish momentum targets surpassing $123,000 resistance in Q4.
data.description: According to the latest Bitcoin technical analysis, there are indications of a bullish breakout in the near future, potentially reaching up to $138,000. The analysis points towards strong momentum indicators that could help push the price above the resistance level of $123,000.

data.title: AM General to showcase new mobile platforms at ASUA 2025; focus on agility & lethality of vehicles. AUSA on Oct. 13-15.
data.description: In South Bend, Indiana, on October 10, 2025, AM General, a top military mobility solutions provider, will emphasize the agility and effectiveness of its main vehicle lineup at the Association of the U.S. Army's Annual Meeting and Exposition from October 13 to 15, 2025. The showcase will take place at Booth #6941.

data.title: JPMorgan to release Q3 earnings, analysts expect $4.86/share (up from $4.37) and $45.42B revenue (up from $42.65B). Dividend yield at 1.96%.
data.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. will announce their third-quarter earnings on Oct. 14, expected to be $4.86 per share compared to $4.37 last year. Revenue for the quarter is projected to be $45.42 billion, up from $42.65 billion a year ago. UBS analyst Erika Najarian reiterated JPMorgan as a Buy with a new price target of $350, up from $339. The current annual dividend yield for JPMorgan is 1.96%, with a quarterly dividend of $1.50 per share ($6.00 annually). Investors are showing interest in potential dividends from the company. More details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Zcash leads privacy coin rally with triple-digit weekly gains as investors flee surveillance worries.
data.description: Privacy-focused cryptocurrencies have shown significant growth over the past week, with gains exceeding 100% as investors flock to assets that provide protection from surveillance and financial restrictions.

data.title: Gold demand surges like Bitcoin; now highest central banks' asset in years. Deutsche Bank suggests influence on Bitcoin.
data.description: The proportion of gold in central bank reserves has surged to its highest level in decades, a development that could influence the role of Bitcoin as a potential reserve asset. Deutsche Bank reported this trend, suggesting that it may have significant implications for the future of both gold and cryptocurrencies.

data.title: Saudi Arabia Diabetic Food Market set to reach US$272.63 million by 2033 with CAGR of 5.42% from 2025 to 2033 due to increased diabetes prevalence and demand for healthier options.
data.description: The report on Saudi Arabia's Diabetic Food Market, available through ResearchAndMarkets.com, projects growth from US$169.54 million in 2024 to US$272.63 million by 2033, indicating a 5.42% CAGR. Factors driving this increase include rising diabetes rates, a surge in health awareness, and a growing preference for sugar-free, low-calorie, and functional food products. The market is segmented by products, distribution channels, states, and company analysis.
